name: Coast Guard Sector Juneau
tagline: The Coast Guard's Alaska district hub in the capital, guarding the rugged Southeast Alaska coast.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Juneau / District 17 (Juneau, AK). Region flagged OCONUS for browse UX, but Alaska is a BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger state -> KEEPS full template (BAH MHA AK402 Juneau; realestate + schools_live work). CG uses same BAH tables. D17 oversees all Alaska CG ops; Juneau is the capital, reachable only by air/ferry (no roads in). Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network/local + medevac. Schools: Juneau SD. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Alaska is TRICARE West. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 99801 (Juneau).

data.Location: Juneau, AK (Southeast Alaska; the state capital)
data.Role: Coast Guard District 17 hub overseeing all Alaska operations
data.Mission: Search and rescue, fisheries, and patrol across Alaska's vast coast
data.Access: Reachable only by air or ferry (no roads connect Juneau)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Juneau anchors District 17, which oversees Coast Guard operations across all of Alaska, from search and rescue to fisheries enforcement along one of the longest, harshest coastlines in the country.

It's in Juneau, Alaska's capital, a stunning Southeast Alaska city wedged between mountains and the sea, and notably reachable only by air or ferry, with no roads connecting it to the rest of the state.

sections.title: A Remote Capital Tour: Embrace Southeast Alaska

data.subtitle: Juneau is beautiful and isolated (no road out). Housing is a small local market, so planning for island-style logistics and the climate matters most.
intro: Juneau is a small, scenic city with a limited housing market and a wet, mild-but-cool maritime climate. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families rent in town.
The big adjustments are the isolation (fly or ferry to leave), higher prices for goods shipped in, and lots of rain, balanced against incredible outdoors at your doorstep.

recommendations.advice: Most families rent in and around Juneau given the small market; start the search early.
No roads out: budget for air/ferry travel and higher prices on shipped goods.
It's a wet maritime climate; good rain gear and an outdoors mindset go a long way.
Glaciers, whales, fishing, and trails are right there; leaning in makes the tour.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing in Juneau is limited, so most families rent in the local market.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office early for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA AK402 (Juneau) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. The Juneau School District serves the city.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: On-base clinic care plus local Juneau hospital and providers; complex care may require medevac to Anchorage, Seattle, or beyond

data.Commissary: Limited; most groceries via local Juneau stores (no large DoD commissary in town)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves. In small, isolated Juneau most families rent, but here's the framework if you're weighing the local market.
rent: Your tour is short or your orders feel uncertain.
You prefer flexibility in a small, isolated market.
You'd rather not own where resale can be slow.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in town.
buy: You expect a longer tour and want to settle in Juneau.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity.
You're comfortable with island-style logistics, weather, and a small resale market.
You've found a home you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, and Juneau's small, isolated market leads most families to rent. If you buy, weigh resale, maintenance, and shipping costs. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics, and a remote Alaska capital adds shipping and travel planning. Start early.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging before you arrive; options are limited. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start the housing search
steps.text: The local market is small; start early and get the housing office's read on Juneau.

steps.title: Plan your vehicle and travel
steps.text: Arrange vehicle shipping/ferry early (no roads into Juneau), and prep for a wet maritime climate. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the Juneau School District early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment; know that complex care can mean medevac off the Southeast, so understand the referral process.

data.a: It's in Alaska, a U.S. state, so it uses stateside pay tools like BAH (not OHA). But it's a remote, fly-or-ferry-only capital, so we group it under the OCONUS/Alaska section for browsing.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. Juneau is MHA AK402.
Juneau is hemmed in by mountains and water; you reach it only by air or ferry, which shapes travel and the cost of shipped goods.
Mostly renting in and around Juneau, since the local market is small and CG housing is limited.
